questions.question: Which at-rule is used to define the stages of a CSS animation?
questions.explanation: The `@keyframes` at-rule defines the sequence of styles that an element transitions through during a CSS animation, using percentage values or the `from`/`to` keywords.

questions.question: What does `animation-fill-mode: forwards` do?
choices: Plays the animation in the forward direction only
Retains the styles from the last keyframe after the animation ends
Starts the animation from the beginning each time
Applies the first keyframe styles before the animation starts
questions.explanation: With `animation-fill-mode: forwards`, the element retains the computed style values from the last keyframe after the animation completes, rather than reverting to its original state.

questions.question: What value of `animation-iteration-count` makes an animation repeat forever?
questions.explanation: Setting `animation-iteration-count: infinite` causes the animation to repeat indefinitely without stopping.

questions.question: What does `animation-direction: alternate` do?
choices: Plays the animation forward on odd iterations and backward on even iterations
Randomly selects the direction each iteration
questions.explanation: With `animation-direction: alternate`, the animation plays forward on odd-numbered iterations and in reverse on even-numbered iterations, creating a back-and-forth effect.

questions.question: In a @keyframes rule, the keyword ___ is equivalent to 0% and defines the starting state of the animation.
questions.explanation: The `from` keyword in a @keyframes rule is an alias for the 0% keyframe, defining the initial state of the animation.

questions.question: The ___ property specifies the name of the @keyframes animation to apply to an element.
questions.explanation: The `animation-name` property links an element to a defined @keyframes rule by referencing its name.
